messages.content:
HOW YOUR CLAIM BECOMES A NUMBER (the perturbation contract):
- You never state € impacts. You emit machine-readable ops; the engine computes
  ΔTAM/ΔSAM/ΔYAM by re-running the real funnel. Any € figure you write in prose
  will be stripped.
- Ops: {nodeId, op: "set"|"scale"|"add", value} rewrite one leaf's value;
  {nodeId, op: "exclude"} removes a dimension cell from the venture's reachable
  scope ("unavailable to us" — foreclosure), which is different from shrinking it.
- Ops apply in array order. Ratio nodes must stay inside [0,1]; tamBase must stay > 0.
- Only FUNNEL-ACTIVE nodes may be perturbed (marked in the ledger you receive).
  shape.cagr and shape.cr3 are context — a demand-pace or concentration risk must
  be translated into the funnel nodes it would actually move (tamBase level,
  serviceableFactor reach, obtainableFactor pace, or a share).
- Sibling shares are NOT re-normalized when you move one share (documented v1
  simplification). If you mean a mix-SHIFT, perturb multiple siblings; if you
  mean "this slice is smaller in absolute terms", one op is correct.
- Perturbing an assumption leaf overrides whatever the user set: your claim is
  about the world, not about their slider.

Fix the hypothesis's targetNodes/perturbation so it passes validation WITHOUT changing the claim's meaning. If the claim cannot be expressed against funnel-active nodes, translate it into the funnel nodes it would actually move.

FUNNEL-ACTIVE NODES:
tamBase (EUR_M, value 300)
geo.DE (ratio, value 0.5)
geo.NL (ratio, value 0.28)
geo.PL (ratio, value 0.07)
geo.CH (ratio, value 0.08)
geo.CZ (ratio, value 0.02)
geo.AT (ratio, value 0.02)
geo.other (ratio, value 0.03)
seg.hyperscale (ratio, value 0.44)
seg.colocation (ratio, value 0.18)
seg.enterprise (ratio, value 0.29)
seg.edge (ratio, value 0.05)
seg.telecom (ratio, value 0.04)
cust.operator-large (ratio, value 0.4)
cust.operator-mid (ratio, value 0.25)
cust.oem (ratio, value 0.22)
cust.distributor (ratio, value 0.13)
serviceableFactor (ratio, value 0.55)
obtainableFactor (ratio, value 0.01)

HYPOTHESIS:
{
 "id": "framework-agreements-foreclose-large-operator-cell",
 "title": "Global framework agreements make the 0.40 large-operator cell mostly non-contestable in the venture's window",
 "narrative": "The model's biggest customer cell — large operators at 0.40 of PDU spend — encodes the assumption 'large operators buy direct, therefore a direct-selling entrant can compete for them.' But the way large operators buy direct is through multi-year global framework agreements and integrated infrastructure packages with incumbents whose customer lists already span every hyperscaler and major colo. A framework doesn't shrink the cell's spend; it forecloses it: the PDU line is decided once, globally, years at a time, and never comes to regional tender. For a pre-launch regional entrant whose profile explicitly targets large operators first, the nominal 0.40 collapses to the sliver of that spend outside framework coverage — while the model's YAM math quietly credits the whole cell as contestable.",
 "category": "competitive",
 "targetNodes": [
  "cust.operator-large"
 ],
 "mechanism": [
  {
   "step": "trigger",
   "text": "Incumbent rack-power vendors hold entrenched direct relationships with the exact buyers in this cell — Vertiv's disclosed major-customer list alone covers Equinix and the global telcos/hyperscalers — and data-center demand is concentrating supplier order books around these accounts",
   "refs": [
    "digest:cust-mix#c12",
    "digest:cust-mix#c0"
   ]
  },
  {
   "step": "propagation",
   "text": "The 0.40 value itself is an unsourced internal estimate: no retrieved source substantiates the channel split, and the one hard number cited (30% of one supplier's orders) measures order composition, not buyer-type contestability — so the cell carries no evidence about how much of it ever reaches open competition",
   "refs": [
    "digest:cust-mix#c0",
    "ledger:cust.operator-large"
   ]
  },
  {
   "step": "model-impact",
   "text": "If framework-covered spend is removed, the large-operator cell available to a new regional entrant in its first 12–24 months is roughly half its nominal size, and the venture's chosen go-to-market points at the most-foreclosed cell first",
   "refs": [
    "ledger:cust.operator-large",
    "profile:Who is the primary buyer you'll sell through first?"
   ]
  }
 ],
 "whyMissable": "'Large operators buy direct' sounds like an access advantage — no distributor margin, a named-account sales motion the founder knows how to run. The foreclosure hides inside the same fact: direct purchasing at scale means framework purchasing, and framework spend never appears as a losable deal, so the pipeline simply looks mysteriously thin rather than visibly blocked.",
 "expectedObviousness": "medium",
 "falsifier": "Three CE large-operator procurement interviews (or won/lost tender records) showing rack-PDU purchases regularly competed line-by-line at regional level outside global frameworks — e.g., annual PDU bake-offs open to non-incumbent vendors.",
 "likelihood": {
  "value": 0.45,
  "rationale": "Framework and integrated-package procurement by large operators is documented behavior in the digest corpus and incumbent customer lists overlap the target accounts almost completely, but the actual framework-coverage share of PDU spend is unquantified — the cell value itself is admitted guesswork in both directions.",
  "basis": "evidence"
 },
 "perturbation": [
  {
   "nodeId": "cust.operator-large",
   "op": "set",
   "value": 0.2,
   "note": "Roughly half of nominal large-operator PDU spend sits under multi-year frameworks/integrated packages and is non-contestable in the venture's window"
  }
 ],
 "indicators": [
  {
   "signal": "New multi-year power-infrastructure framework or integrated-rack supply announcements naming CE operators",
   "where": "Schneider/Vertiv/Eaton quarterly earnings calls and press rooms; operator capex-day presentations",
   "threshold": "≥1 new CE-relevant operator signing a global power framework covering rack distribution",
   "updates": "increases"
  },
  {
   "signal": "Standalone rack-PDU lots appearing (or not) in operator tenders, and their bidder counts",
   "where": "TED (EU tender portal) and operator supplier portals for DE/NL colo fit-outs",
   "threshold": "Standalone PDU lots absent, sole-sourced, or drawing ≤2 bidders across two consecutive quarters",
   "updates": "increases"
  }
 ],
 "mitigations": [
  {
   "action": "Commission the three CE operator/distributor procurement interviews already named in the ledger's promotionPath, specifically instrumented to measure framework coverage of PDU spend",
   "type": "information",
   "voiNodeId": "cust.operator-large",
   "note": "Replaces the unsourced 0.40 with a contestability-adjusted split"
  },
  {
   "action": "Re-sequence go-to-market to lead with colocation mid-market and OEM/integrator channels where line-item bake-offs still occur, holding large operators as a Year-2+ motion entered via a framework-holder partnership",
   "type": "strategic",
   "voiNodeId": null,
   "note": null
  }
 ],
 "lens": "execution-window",
 "riskId": "risk.execution-window.framework-agreements-foreclose-large-operator-cell"
}

VALIDATION ERROR:
[
  {
    "code": "invalid_value",
    "values": [
      "error",
      "risk"
    ],
    "path": [
      0,
      "resolution"
    ],
    "message": "Invalid option: expected one of \"error\"|\"risk\""
  }
]

Emit the corrected hypothesis JSON (same shape).

But the way large operators buy direct is through multi-year global framework agreements and integrated infrastructure packages with incumbents whose customer lists already span every hyperscaler and major colo. A framework doesn't shrink the cell's spend; it forecloses it: the PDU line is decided once, globally, years at a time, and never comes to regional tender. operator capex-day presentations","threshold":"≥1 new CE-relevant operator signing a global power framework covering rack distribution","updates":"increases"},{"signal":"Standalone rack-PDU lots appearing (or not) in operator tenders, and their bidder counts","where":"TED (EU tender portal) and operator supplier portals for DE/NL colo fit-outs","threshold":"Standalone PDU lots absent, sole-sourced, or drawing ≤2 bidders across two consecutive quarters","updates":"increases"}],"mitigations":[{"action":"Commission the three CE operator/distributor procurement interviews already named in the ledger's promotionPath, specifically instrumented to measure framework coverage of PDU spend","type":"information","voiNodeId":"cust.operator-large","note":"Replaces the unsourced 0.40 with a contestability-adjusted split"},{"action":"Re-sequence go-to-market to lead with colocation mid-market and OEM/integrator channels where line-item bake-offs still occur, holding large operators as a Year-2+ motion entered via a framework-holder partnership","type":"strategic","voiNodeId":null,"note":null}]}
